description: Modular component of the investment analyst research suite. Scrapes ECB speeches, encodes them into a SQLite vector database, scores per-speaker hawkishness via Claude (file-based handoff), and generates a self-contained HTML dashboard.
inference_method: file-based handoff — analyzer.py writes prompt files to ecb_hawkometer/data/prompts/, OpenCode reads them and writes JSON results to ecb_hawkometer/data/results/, dashboard.py consumes the JSON
trigger: Phrase 'show me the ECB speaker dashboard' routed via existing router.py as intent ECB_HAWKOMETER

features.name: Project Scaffold & Dependencies
features.description: Create the ecb_hawkometer/ directory structure, requirements.txt with all dependencies, and verify the virtual environment can install them using the PGGM internal PyPI with trusted-host config.
features.test: python -c "import requests, bs4, sentence_transformers, dateutil; print('All imports OK')"
acceptance_criteria: Directory structure exists
All packages import without error inside .venv

features.name: Scraper
features.description: scraper.py fetches all ECB speeches from the past 12 months from https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/key/html/index.en.html. Performs delta refresh — only scrapes speeches not already in the database. Extracts: speaker, date, title, full text (HTML→plain), URL. Defensive selectors with clear error messages if the page structure changes.
acceptance_criteria: Returns a list of dicts with keys: speaker, date, title, full_text, url
Skips URLs already present in the database (delta refresh)
Does not crash if ECB page structure changes — prints a clear warning instead
Handles network errors gracefully with retry logic

features.name: Vector Database
features.description: db.py manages a SQLite database (ecb_hawkometer/data/ecb_speeches.db). Stores speeches with embeddings generated by sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2). Exposes: upsert_speech(), get_speeches(speaker, date_from, date_to), get_all_speakers(), speech_exists(url). Embeddings stored as BLOB. No external vector DB required.

columns.speaker: TEXT
columns.title: TEXT
columns.embedding: BLOB (numpy float32 array serialised with numpy.tobytes)

acceptance_criteria: Creates DB and table on first run
upsert_speech() inserts new speeches and skips duplicates
get_speeches() returns correct subset filtered by speaker and date range
Embedding round-trips correctly (store then retrieve gives same vector shape)

features.name: Speaker Weights & Inference Analyzer
features.description: weights.py contains the speaker tier/weight registry as a Python dict. analyzer.py implements the file-based handoff pattern: (A) builds structured prompt files for per-speaker hawkishness scoring and (B) policy prediction, writes them to ecb_hawkometer/data/prompts/, then reads the JSON results OpenCode writes to ecb_hawkometer/data/results/. Exposes get_speaker_scores() and get_policy_prediction() as importable functions.

task_A_output_schema_dashboard_ui.speaker: string
key_themes: list of strings — rendered as bright chips in speaker board and deep dive
tone_keywords: list of strings — rendered as dimmer chips in speaker board and deep dive
task_A_output_schema_dashboard_ui.stance_signal: hawkish | neutral-hawkish tilt | neutral | dovish | off-mandate — rendered as coloured badge
task_A_output_schema_dashboard_ui.tone_summary: string — DEPRECATED IN UI: not rendered in dashboard, kept in JSON for reference
task_A_output_schema_dashboard_ui.representative_quote: string — DEPRECATED IN UI: not rendered in dashboard, kept in JSON for reference

acceptance_criteria: weights.py dict covers all 4 tiers with correct weights (1.0, 0.8, 0.6, 0.4)
analyzer.py writes well-formed prompt files
get_speaker_scores() returns list of dicts matching Task A output schema
get_policy_prediction() returns dict matching Task B output schema
Both functions handle missing result files gracefully with a clear error message

features.name: HTML Dashboard Generator
features.description: dashboard.py reads the JSON output from analyzer.py and generates a single self-contained ecb_hawkometer/output/ecb_dashboard.html file. Opens it in the default browser via webbrowser.open(). Dark theme, financial terminal aesthetic. All CSS/JS inline — no external dependencies.
sections: Header: title, generated timestamp, next meeting date + countdown
Section 1: Speaker Hawkishness Board — sortable table/card grid with score bar, trend arrow, theme tags, color scale blue→grey→red
Section 2: Per-Speaker Deep Dive — expandable panel with tone summary, representative quote, recent speeches list, sparkline if 3+ datapoints
Section 3: Policy Prediction — HIKE/CUT/HOLD verdict, confidence badge, weighted score gauge, rubric table, rationale paragraph
acceptance_criteria: Generates valid HTML file
File is self-contained — no external CSS/JS URLs
Opens in browser without errors
Renders correctly with mock data (no real API calls needed for this test)
Sortable table works via inline JavaScript

features.name: Router Integration & Orchestration
features.description: Integrates ECB_HAWKOMETER as a new intent in the existing router.py. The trigger phrase 'show me the ECB speaker dashboard' (and variants) is classified and dispatched to ecb_hawkometer/main.py which runs the full pipeline: scrape → store → analyze → dashboard → open browser. The orchestration script also checks for pending prompt files and coordinates with OpenCode for inference.
pipeline_steps: 3. analyzer.py — write prompt files for Task A (per speaker) and Task B (policy prediction)
4. PAUSE: print message to user that prompt files are ready for OpenCode inference
5. analyzer.py — read result JSON files written by OpenCode
acceptance_criteria: router.py correctly classifies 'show me the ECB speaker dashboard' as ECB_HAWKOMETER
intents/ecb_hawkometer.py run() calls ecb_hawkometer/main.py pipeline
Pipeline runs end-to-end with mock data without crashing
Clear status messages printed at each step
Output matches parent project output format conventions (source + vintage footer)

notes: F1 must complete before any other feature (directories must exist)
F2 and F3 can run in parallel after F1
F4 depends on F3 (needs db.py API), F5 can run in parallel with F4
F6 depends on all prior features

last_week_speech_schema.description: Schema for last_week_*.json inference results — one object per speech

fields.speaker: string
fields.title: string
fields.shift_label: string — rendered as coloured shift badge
fields.tone_comparison: string — semantic comparison sentence shown below chips
fields.representative_quote: string — DEPRECATED IN UI: not rendered
